_meta.note: Starter intents. Edit me! 'keywords' are synonym-expanded; 'examples' are matched by meaning. An intent with 'requires' is a COMPOSITE: it only fires when ALL listed concepts appear together, and it outranks the single-concept intents (this is how the brain combines intents into one answer). 'requires' values can be plain words or context tags like @morning, @winter, @rainy.

responses: I'm not sure what you mean yet. Try 'help' to see what I know, or teach me the answer.
Hmm, that's a new one. Type the answer and I'll remember it for next time.
Hi — what can I do for you?
I'm a tiny intent brain. I work out what you mean — even with different wording — and answer. Open brain_intents.json to teach me your own intents.
I can tell the time and date, do quick math, greet you, and more. Try: 'what time is it', '12 times 8', or combine them: 'the time and the date'. Everything I know lives in brain_intents.json.
Here's where your plans would go — wire this intent up to your own calendar or to-do list in brain_intents.json.
I can't read a live forecast in this demo — but tell me the conditions (e.g. "it's pouring outside") and I'll remember them.

examples: should I plan around the weather
plan my day based on the forecast
responses: Planning around the weather, are we? This is a COMPOSITE intent — it only fired because you mentioned both a plan AND the weather. Neither word alone would trigger it.
